stories.headline: Mystery model Hy3 is crushing GPT-4o on OpenRouter's leaderboard
stories.blurb: An unidentified LLM called Hy3 has shot to the top of OpenRouter's user preference rankings, beating established models by a significant margin. No one knows who built it, what architecture it uses, or how it's trained—but developers are routing real queries to it.
stories.why_it_matters: A completely anonymous AI model is now handling production workloads based purely on performance.
stories.original_headline: The mysterious Hy3 LLM is topping OpenRouter Model Rankings by a large margin

stories.headline: You can now run AI chatbots directly on your iPhone
stories.blurb: Several apps let you download and run AI models locally on your iPhone — no internet required. Your conversations stay on your device, and the chatbot works offline. The tradeoff: local models are smaller and less capable than cloud versions like ChatGPT.
stories.why_it_matters: Privacy-conscious users get AI without sending data to servers.
stories.original_headline: How to run a local AI chatbot on your iPhone

stories.headline: Anthropic just released Claude Opus 4.8, its most capable model yet
stories.blurb: Claude Opus 4.8 is now available across all tiers. Anthropic says it outperforms the previous flagship on complex reasoning, coding, and extended context tasks, while maintaining the same speed and pricing as Opus 4.
stories.why_it_matters: The new flagship raises the bar for what production AI can handle without changing your budget.

stories.headline: BMW puts humanoid robots on its assembly line in Europe
stories.blurb: BMW is deploying humanoid robots at a European car plant, expanding a trial that started in the US. The robots are designed to work alongside humans on tasks like installing door seals and moving parts—jobs that are physically demanding or repetitive.
stories.why_it_matters: Humanoid form factor means existing factory layouts don't need rebuilding for automation.
stories.original_headline: Humanoid robots 'the future' of car making, says BMW

try_this_prompt.title: The jargon translator
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm about to paste something I wrote that sounds too clever or too internal. Rewrite it so a smart outsider—someone who doesn't work here—gets it in one read. Keep my point, lose the acronyms and insider shorthand. If a sentence needs a glossary, kill it.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before sending anything to a new stakeholder, contractor, or your future self in six months.

daily_question: If you could put one AI model or agent on a factory floor today, what would it do?
notes: Cross slot has no valid candidates. Shortlist options: Waymo (vehicle design, AI incidental), BMW humanoid robots (adoption story, not AI capability), Oura Ring 5 (hardware form factor), Airbnb robot disaster (AI failure, violates north star). Scout already flagged gaps in frontier API/tool launches and coding-agent updates. Shipping 3 stories: Claude Opus 4.8 (engineer), iPhone local chatbot (beginner), Hy3 mystery model (business). Adjustments: Skipping cross slot entirely. Three strong Tier 1 stories that pass rubric: major model launch, practical mobile AI how-to, and competitive market detective story.
